open_questions: Contract backfill 2026-07-21: four additive components with valid calculation proofs; component sum **~$125/sh** vs **~$130** price.
Q1 2026 margin compression vs revenue growth. Confirm management guidance on FY2026 operating margin.
Convertible notes **$4.14B** principal (~$22/sh net debt claim). Equity conversion path not in base proof.
Incentive plan expansion (+8M shares) approved May 2026. Dilution watch.
No third-party sources indexed; Marvin floor only.
thesis: Akamai Technologies operates a global edge platform that delivers web content, blocks cyberattacks, and runs cloud compute workloads for enterprises. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$4.21 billion** (+5.4% versus last year) across three solution lines: Compute (**$2.24 billion**), Security (**$1.26 billion**), and Delivery (**$708 million**). **[Fact]** Customers pay subscription and usage fees for CDN traffic, security bundles (WAF, bot management, zero trust), and cloud infrastructure (including Linode and GPU services). The business is capital-intensive at the network layer but generates substantial cash: **$1.52 billion** cash from operations in FY2025.
why_market_wrong: The stock trades like a mature CDN utility (**~19 times** FY2025 free cash flow per share) while more than half of revenue now comes from faster-growing Compute. **[Inference]** Q1 2026 operating profit fell **26%** year-over-year even as revenue rose **5.8%**, so the market may be correctly punishing near-term margin compression from cloud build-out. **[Inference]** If compute mix sustains high-single-digit growth and margins stabilize after lease and capex digestion, today's price could understate a platform transitioning from legacy delivery to edge cloud. No dated catalyst; mispricing read is **quality transition at a compressed multiple**, not an equity yield curve.
